name: Draft Slot Tracker
description: Searchable web tracker of every MLB draft signing 2018-2025 (~4,900 picks): bonus vs slot value, over/under-slot diff, share of team bonus pool, with filters for year, round range, position (RHP/LHP split), college vs HS, power conference, age at draft, and bonus range. Exports the filtered view to CSV; a companion Node script turns that export into a styled one-page PDF report.
stack: Static HTML/CSS/JS (single index.html, no framework, no build step)
Node + headless Chrome (make-pdf.js, local PDF reports)
hosting: GitHub Pages, deployed automatically from main of Stadium-Ventures/draft-slot-tracker
access: Public URL, no auth — repo and site are public. On-page brand reads "SV Draft Slots".

inputs: data.csv committed in the repo — a static snapshot of MLB draft signings 2018-2025 (~4,900 rows: pick, player, bonus, slot value, position, bats/throws, school/conference, birthdate). No live feed; the page fetches this file client-side.
outputs: CSV download of the current filtered view (Export CSV button on the site)
One-page PDF reports generated locally: node make-pdf.js <export.csv> [title] (renders via headless Google Chrome)

links.label: Live site
links.note: MLB Draft Slot Tracker (2018-2025). Search a player or filter, then Export CSV for report material.

monitoring: None needed — static GitHub Pages site with no scheduled jobs. Only failure mode is a Pages build failure on push, visible in the repo's Actions tab ("pages build and deployment").
knownIssues: Data is a frozen snapshot through the 2025 draft — 2026 signings are not in it; adding them means updating data.csv by hand (👤 Mike DeCicco)
notes: Repo was transferred from mdecicco-SV/draft-slot-tracker to the Stadium-Ventures org; the old name is now a GitHub redirect, so either remote URL works but Stadium-Ventures/draft-slot-tracker is canonical. Open question: the original source/export behind data.csv (columns suggest an MLB draft database export) is not documented in-repo, so refreshing the data requires re-deriving that pull.
runbook: Clone Stadium-Ventures/draft-slot-tracker — no dependencies, no env vars, no secrets.
Run locally: python3 -m http.server 8000 in the repo root, then open http://localhost:8000 (index.html fetches data.csv, so opening the file directly via file:// will not load data).
Update data: edit data.csv (header row defines the schema: phase,year,draftRound,overall,...,bonus,slot_bonus,difference), commit, push to main.
Deploy: push to main — GitHub Pages rebuilds automatically in ~1 minute (watch the "pages build and deployment" run in the repo's Actions tab).
PDF report: use the site's Export CSV button, then node make-pdf.js <export.csv> [title] [output.pdf] — requires Google Chrome at the default macOS path, or set CHROME=/path/to/chrome.
First troubleshooting step if the site looks stale or broken: check the latest "pages build and deployment" run under the repo's Actions tab.
